zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Elizabeth Beauchamp於1468年1月27日出生在英國武斯特斯爾時，她的父親Richard Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp34歲，母親Elizabeth Stafford44歲。她於1491年2月28日與Robert de Broke Willoughby Sir, 12th ggf在英國維爾爾維斯波特 聖 瑪麗結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1503年8月10日在英國英格蘭戴文享年35歲，埋葬在英國戴文。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Elizabeth Beauchamp于1468年1月27日出生在英国武斯特斯爾时，她的父亲Richard Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp34岁，母亲Elizabeth Stafford44岁。她于1491年2月28日与Robert de Broke Willoughby Sir, 12th ggf在英国維爾爾維斯波特 聖 瑪麗结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1503年8月10日在英国英格兰戴文享年35岁，埋葬在英国戴文。

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Beachum
meanings: Altered form of English   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: English:
possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Polish: variant of   Lada  .
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: Of uncertain origin. If not simply an invention, it may have been devised as a feminine equivalent of   Alan   (of which it is an anagram), or a shortened form of   Alana .  It seems to have been first used by the American film actress  Lana Turner  (  1920–1995 ), whose original name was  Julia .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Elizabeth Beauchamp was born on 27 January 1468, in Worcestershire, England, her father, Richard Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp, was 34 and her mother, Elizabeth Stafford, was 44. She married Robert de Broke Willoughby Sir, 12th ggf on 28 February 1491, in Westport St Mary, Wiltshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died on 10 August 1503, in Devon, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 35, and was buried in Devon, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Elizabeth Beauchamp est née le 27 janvier 1468, à Worcestershire, Angleterre, son père, Richard Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Stafford, avait 44 ans. Elle a épousé Robert de Broke Willoughby Sir, 12th ggf le 28 février 1491, à Westport St Mary, Wiltshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée le 10 août 1503, à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 35 ans, et a été enterrée à Devon, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Elizabeth Beauchamp am 27. Januar 1468 in Worcestershire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp 34 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Stafford 44. Sie hat am 28. Februar 1491 in Westport St Mary, Wiltshire, England Robert de Broke Willoughby Sir, 12th ggf geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb am 10. August 1503 im Alter von 35 Jahren in Devon, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Devon, England begraben.

Lei sposato Robert de Broke Willoughby Sir, 12th ggf il 28 febbraio 1491, nel Westport St Mary, Wiltshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. ja.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Beauchampが1468年1月27日にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアで生まれた時，父Richard Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchampは34歳，母Elizabeth Staffordは44歳であった。1491年2月28日にイギリスウィルトシュアイアウエストプオルト スト マリーでRobert de Broke Willoughby Sir, 12th ggfと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1503年8月10日にイギリスイギリスデボンで35歳で死去し，イギリスデボンにて埋葬された。

ko.Ottery St Mary, Devon, England, United K: 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 오터이 스트 메리
ko.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Beauchamp(이)가 1468년 1월 27일 영국 워스스터실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Stafford(은)는 44세였다. 그녀는 1491년 2월 28일 영국 윌츠히르 웨스트포트 스트 메리에서 Robert de Broke Willoughby Sir, 12th ggf(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1503년 8월 10일 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에서 35세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 데폰에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Elizabeth Beauchamp nasceu em 27 de janeiro de 1468, em Worcestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Stafford, tinha 44 anos. Ela casou-se com Robert de Broke Willoughby Sir, 12th ggf em 28 de fevereiro de 1491, em Westport St Mary, Wiltshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 10 de agosto de 1503, em Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 35 anos, e foi sepultada em Devon, Inglaterra.

Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert de Broke Willoughby Sir, 12th ggf 28 февраля 1491 г. в местности вестпорт ст мэри, уилцхир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла 10 августа 1503 г. в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 35 лет и была похоронена в местности девон, Англия.
